Dissented in Obergefell v. Hodges (2015), rejecting a constitutional right to same-sex marriage, arguing the Constitution is silent on marriage and its definition belongs to the people of the states, and warning the decision would be used to stigmatize Americans who hold the man-woman view of marriage.

candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services
candidates.notes: RFK Jr. scores the lowest on this scorecard. Despite deep Catholic upbringing (rosary multiple times daily, mass twice daily in summers), his positions align with liberal Catholicism on most issues. His prior pro-abortion views drew opposition from Mike Pence's group during confirmation. He was confirmed 52-48 with only Mitch McConnell voting against among Republicans. His focus is on vaccine skepticism and health freedom, not the social conservative issues measured here. The Kennedy family legacy includes strong gun control advocacy. His environmental career was internationally focused. His Catholic faith is genuine but expressed through social justice rather than social conservatism. [2026 actions: As HHS Secretary, cut universally-recommended vaccines from 17 to 11 (January 2026; federal judge blocked overhaul March 16, 2026). FDA under his direction blocked publication of safety studies on COVID + shingles vaccines (May 2026). Hired David Geier to study vaccine-autism link. Confirms strong T on industry_capture[q0] (anti-mandate) + [q1] (anti-pharma-shield agenda).]

candidates.notes: Former Vice President (2017-2021), former Governor of Indiana (2013-2017), former US Rep (2001-2013). Evangelical Christian. Signed RFRA Indiana 2015. Certified 2020 Electoral College vote 1/6/2021 against Trump pressure. 2024 R presidential candidate (withdrew).

profile.religion: Christian (Evangelical) — College Park Church, Indianapolis IN

candidates.office: Former U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations (2017-2018)
candidates.notes: Former US Ambassador to the UN (2017-2018, Trump T1), former two-term Governor of SC (2011-2017). 2024 R presidential candidate (came closest to Trump in primaries). Methodist convert from Sikh upbringing. Hawkish foreign-policy lean.

candidates.name: Todd Blanche
candidates.office: U.S. Acting Attorney General (since April 2026); Deputy AG since January 2025
candidates.notes: Acting U.S. Attorney General since April 2, 2026, after President Trump removed Pam Bondi. Previously Deputy Attorney General (confirmed Senate 52-46 on March 5, 2025). Born 1974 in Denver CO, Brooklyn Law 2003. Spent 8 years at US Attorney's Office (SDNY) violent-crimes division, then Cadwalader partner. Best known as Donald Trump's personal defense attorney in his criminal trials (NY hush-money, Mar-a-Lago documents) before joining the administration. Most v4.0 categories left as unscored since he has not held elected office or taken broad public-policy positions on those questions — he is primarily a litigator-turned-administrator. [2026 actions: Acting AG since April 2, 2026. Intensified investigation into former CIA Dir John Brennan + grand conspiracy theory (appointed Joseph diGenova to lead); approved investigations into Cassidy Hutchinson, ActBlue, Southern Poverty Law Center. CPAC speech: FBI "cleaned house" — no agents who worked on Trump cases still employed. DOJ explicitly aligned to President Trump as "primary client" per first-press-conference framing.]

candidates.name: Kristi Noem
candidates.office: U.S. Special Envoy for the Shield of the Americas (since March 5, 2026); former Secretary of Homeland Security
candidates.notes: Special Envoy for the Shield of the Americas since March 5, 2026, a new initiative to coordinate Western Hemisphere security (drug cartels, trafficking, illegal migration). Previously Secretary of Homeland Security (Jan 2025 – March 2026) — removed by Trump after congressional hearings drew bipartisan criticism. Earlier: Governor of South Dakota (2019-2025, two terms), US House SD-AL (2011-2019). Strong pro-life + 2A + parental rights record. Hawkish on foreign policy.

candidates.name: Sean Duffy
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Transportation (sworn January 28, 2025; 20th Sec)
candidates.notes: Sworn in January 28, 2025 as 20th US Secretary of Transportation (confirmed 77-22). Also served as acting NASA Administrator July-Dec 2025 (succeeded by Jared Isaacman). Former US House WI-07 (2011-2019), Fox Business host, MTV "Real World" cast member. Catholic, married to former Fox News host Rachel Campos-Duffy. [2026 actions: As SecTrans + acting NASA Administrator (interim between Jared Isaacman tenure), unveiled new ATC system (Peraton contract, completion 2028); FAA reorganization largest in agency history; eVTOL/advanced air mobility pilot program (8 selections); 50% of FY2026 controller-hiring goal hit by April. Low-controversy profile; standard MAGA-conservative archetype.]

candidates.name: Doug Collins
candidates.office: U.S. Secretary of Veterans Affairs (sworn February 5, 2025)
candidates.notes: Sworn in as Secretary of Veterans Affairs February 5, 2025. Ordained Southern Baptist pastor; USAF Reserve chaplain (Lt Col). Former US House GA-09 (2013-2021). Led Trump T1 House impeachment defense. Pastor-veteran combo gives him strong personal cred on religious + military service. Has cut 30K+ VA positions in T2 reorganization; testified Feb 2026 clashed with Dems over VA streamlining; 100K new veterans signed up for VA health care following improvements.

candidates.name: Russ Vought
candidates.office: Director of the Office of Management and Budget (sworn February 7, 2025); Acting Director, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
candidates.notes: Sworn in as Director of OMB February 7, 2025. Also acting CFPB Director. Trump T1 OMB Director. Founder/President of Center for Renewing America. Lead architect of Project 2025 and the "Mandate for Leadership." Evangelical Christian, member of New City Church (Arlington VA). Has overseen large-scale federal workforce reductions and agency restructurings. Among the most ideologically MAGA-aligned cabinet officials.

claims.text: A devout Christian who attended the 2020 March for Life and praised Trump's participation as 'a golden chapter for our movement.' As OMB Director he has called for banning mifepristone and other drugs used in medical abortions — affirming that human life deserves protection from conception.

Publicly described transgender identity as a 'contagion' and has supported executive policies rolling back gender-identity language from federal regulations, rejecting transgender ideology in federal programs — matching the rubric's standard.

As OMB Director in both Trump terms, Vought has been a consistent advocate for reducing the federal deficit, opposing large unauthorized spending packages, and cutting agency budgets. He dismantled the CFPB and directed a broad federal spending-reduction agenda consistent with the rubric's anti-deficit, fiscal-restraint standard.

candidates.name: Kash Patel
candidates.office: Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (sworn February 21, 2025)
candidates.notes: Confirmed by Senate to lead the FBI under Trump T2 (sworn Feb 2025). Also acting ATF Director briefly. Former Department of Defense Chief of Staff (Trump T1). Author of "Government Gangsters" detailing alleged FBI/DOJ abuses. Hindu. Indian-American. Tied job security at FBI to purging agents linked to Trump probes (per fired acting FBI chief Brian Driscoll testimony May 2026).

candidates.name: Stephen Miller
candidates.office: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy and Homeland Security Advisor
candidates.notes: White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy + Homeland Security Advisor in Trump T2. Trump T1 senior policy advisor. Founder of America First Legal. Lead architect of Trump immigration policy (travel ban, family separation, asylum restrictions). Jewish. Among the most influential non-cabinet officials in the Trump administration; signature issue is immigration restriction.

claims.text: The principal architect of the Trump administration's mass-deportation program. Miller described the 2025 deportation campaign as rivaling 'building the Panama Canal' in scope, drove zero-tolerance and mandatory-detention policies across both Trump terms, and in 2025 publicly floated suspending habeas corpus for immigrants to accelerate removals — a hard-enforcement posture matching the rubric ideal.

A key policy driver behind executive actions reinstating the ban on transgender military service and mandating that federal agencies recognize only two biological sexes. Miller's America First policy portfolio consistently opposes the expansion of gender ideology into federal law and institutions.

candidates.name: Mike Waltz
candidates.office: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations (sworn 2025 after Senate confirmation; former NSA Jan-May 2025; former US House FL-06 2019-2025)
candidates.notes: Sworn in as US Ambassador to the United Nations 2025 (filled last remaining T2 cabinet vacancy after 8-month delay). Previously: National Security Advisor (Jan-May 2025) — ousted following "Signalgate" incident where he mistakenly added Atlantic editor Jeffrey Goldberg to a Signal chat coordinating military strike on Houthis. US House FL-06 (2019-2025). Green Beret, three-tour combat veteran. Catholic. Pro-Israel hawk; defense-establishment-aligned.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-18 web-validated: UN Ambassador since 2025; former NSA Jan-May 2025; ousted following Signalgate.

claims.text: Earned a 100% rating from the National Right to Life Committee (2023-24) and voted for HR 26, the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act, in 2023. Stated publicly that Roe v. Wade was 'overreaching and unconstitutional legislating from the Supreme Court,' affirming the pro-life position that life deserves protection before birth.

NRA-endorsed since his first election in 2018; the NRA called him 'a staunch supporter of the Second Amendment' who would 'work hard to protect our constitutional freedoms.' Consistent pro-gun voting record throughout six years in the House (FL-06, 2019-2025).

As U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations (confirmed September 2025), Waltz declared he would seek fundamental reform of the United Nations, stating the body had 'drifted from its core mission of peacemaking' — a posture aligned with the rubric's opposition to UN institutional expansion and entanglement.

candidates.name: Mac Warner
candidates.office: Acting U.S. Assistant Attorney General for the Civil Rights Division (since January 2025); former WV Secretary of State (2017-2025)
candidates.notes: Acting U.S. Assistant Attorney General for the Civil Rights Division since January 2025 (under Trump T2 DOJ). Former WV Secretary of State 2017-2025. WVU School of Law graduate, US Army JAG Corps veteran (Lt Col), three sons all military veterans.
